title: Sprint 3 planning on the rollout of the payment fix, reconciliation, and canary of the alternative provider.

metadata.focus: Start of sprint 3 with stable access to reconciliation, rollout of the fix, and change of stance on the provider.

segments.speaker: Ane
segments.text: We're entering sprint 3. I want this sprint to be about closing things that have been open for too long without coming up with a pretty ending that won't hold up later.

segments.speaker: Nora
segments.text: Good news first: finance has finally given us stable access to the settlement export. We no longer depend on requesting a CSV manually every time.

segments.speaker: Ane
segments.text: Perfect. So we can consider the access request closed and the main blockage for reconciliation disappears.

segments.speaker: Mikel
segments.text: With that, we can really do a minimum reconciliation. Not just looking at cases manually, but cross-referencing attempts, settlements, and the provider's results.

segments.speaker: Jon
segments.text: And the timeout fix is still open. The idempotency key part is done, but we need to extend the rollout to the wallets adapter and measure it with real traffic.

segments.speaker: Leire
segments.text: In the frontend, we also need to better instrument the wallet callbacks because there are still some weird states showing up.

segments.speaker: Ane
segments.text: Okay, that commitment doesn't go away: it gets refined and is taken to a progressive rollout on wallets.

segments.speaker: Mikel
segments.text: Regarding the provider, with the comparison in hand and the freeze already over, I wouldn't wait until sprint 4 to test something. I propose opening a canary for the alternative provider at five percent of the wallet traffic.

segments.speaker: Jon
segments.text: That changes what we said in the last review. There we agreed to keep the current provider throughout this sprint and only prepare the canary for later.

segments.speaker: Ane
segments.text: Correct. If we take that step, it needs to be treated as a decision change, not just simple continuity.

segments.speaker: Leire
segments.text: I'm on board if the canary is limited to wallets and if we have a clear rollback. I wouldn't buy a broader change without that safety net.

segments.speaker: Nora
segments.text: And in the meantime, I want reconciliation to stop being artisanal. If we can detect real duplicates in less than an hour, support gains a lot.

segments.speaker: Ane
segments.text: So the three fronts of the sprint are: progressive rollout of the fix in wallets, minimum automatable reconciliation, and a very controlled canary of the alternative provider.

segments.speaker: Ane
segments.text: Let's put the new decision in writing: we open a limited canary in sprint 3 instead of waiting for the next one, because now we have comparison, observability, and the end of the freeze.

expected_items.title: Extend the rollout of the retry fix to the wallets adapter and measure its real impact.
expected_items.summary: Deploy the already implemented fix to wallet traffic and monitor its behavior with real data.

expected_items.title: Automate a minimal reconciliation of duplicate payments with the stable settlement export.
expected_items.summary: Cross attempts, settlements, and provider results to stop resolving duplicates manually.

expected_items.title: Launch a canary of the alternative provider on 5 percent of wallet traffic.
expected_items.summary: Test a limited alternative provider with a clear rollback and complete observability.

expected_items.title: Open the canary of the alternative provider during sprint 3 instead of waiting for the next.
expected_items.summary: The team changes the previous stance because it now has a comparison, observability, and the end of the contractual freeze.

expected_followups.matched_history_title: Request stable access to the settlement export to unblock the reconciliation of duplicates.
Analyze incidents of duplicate charges and define a minimal reconciliation.
Correct the retry strategy of the payment processing endpoint with exponential backoff.
Keep the current provider during sprint 3 with weekly SLO tracking and prepare a technical canary after the freeze.
